reasoning: The linear trend tool predicts SPG's stock price will revert somewhat over the next month in part because the current price of 201.5 is well above the recent trend, predicting a close of 198.5 on March 8th and P(exceeding 201.5) = 0.40. Importantly, SPG goes ex-dividend on March 10, 2026, meaning its stock price will inherently drop by ~$2.20 per share on the open that day, putting downward pressure on the stock ahead of March 31. With a negative predicted drift + an ex-div drop, the odds of SPG closing higher than the Feb 27 price is lower than 50/50. I assign a probability of 0.48 to March 8 and 0.48 to March 31 (due to increased uncertainty, mitigating the negative ex-div effect).
